channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Takeaways from Hillary Clinton's deposition. And, Paramount outbids for Warner Bros.
item.description: Hillary Clinton says the questioning in a closed-door deposition with the House Oversight Committee's Epstein investigation was repetitive. And, Paramount has outbid Netflix for Warner Bros.
item.content:encoded:
Hillary Clinton says the questioning in a closed-door deposition with the House Oversight Committee's Epstein investigation was repetitive. And, Paramount has outbid Netflix for Warner Bros.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: My doctor keeps focusing on my weight. What other health metrics matter more?
item.description: Our Real Talk with a Doc columnist explains how to push back if your doctor's obsessed with weight loss. And what other health metrics matter more instead.
item.content:encoded: Our Real Talk with a Doc columnist explains how to push back if your doctor's obsessed with weight loss. And what other health metrics matter more instead.
item.dc:creator: Mara Gordon

item.title: Forget the State of the Union. What's the state of your quiz score?
item.description: What's the state of your union, quiz-wise? Find out!
item.content:encoded: What's the state of your union, quiz-wise? Find out!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: As the U.S. celebrates its 250th birthday, many Latinos question whether they belong
item.description: Many U.S.-born Latinos feel afraid and anxious amid the political rhetoric. Still, others wouldn't miss celebrating their country
item.content:encoded:
Many U.S.-born Latinos feel afraid and anxious amid the political rhetoric. Still, others wouldn't miss celebrating their country

(Image credit: Ilana Panich-Linsman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza

item.title: A team of midlife cheerleaders in Ukraine refuses to let war defeat them
item.description: Ukrainian women in their 50s and 60s say they've embraced cheerleading as a way to cope with the extreme stress and anxiety of four years of Russia's full-scale invasion.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian women in their 50s and 60s say they've embraced cheerleading as a way to cope with the extreme stress and anxiety of four years of Russia's full-scale invasion.

(Image credit: Anton Shtuka for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: SNL mocked her as a 'scary mom.' In the Senate, Katie Britt is an emerging dealmaker
item.description: Sen. Katie Britt, Republican of Alabama, is a budding bipartisan dealmaker. Her latest assignment: helping negotiate changes to immigration enforcement tactics.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Katie Britt, Republican of Alabama, is a budding bipartisan dealmaker. Her latest assignment: helping negotiate changes to immigration enforcement tactics.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Nancy Guthrie case: How do families of missing people cope with the uncertainty?
item.description: When a loved one goes missing, relatives can feel guilty simply for eating, says Charlie Shunick, whose sister was kidnapped. Shunick now helps others navigate a nightmare "nobody is prepared for."
item.content:encoded:
When a loved one goes missing, relatives can feel guilty simply for eating, says Charlie Shunick, whose sister was kidnapped. Shunick now helps others navigate a nightmare "nobody is prepared for."

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: How a 3-week babysitting gig turned into a lifelong relationship for two women
item.description: Margaret Tobin accepted a three-week babysitting gig in 1989 for a newborn named Audrey that turned into a life-long relationship. The two women talk about their life together.
item.content:encoded: Margaret Tobin accepted a three-week babysitting gig in 1989 for a newborn named Audrey that turned into a life-long relationship. The two women talk about their life together.
item.dc:creator: Zanna McKay

item.title: This community festival embraces the joys of a frozen lake — while it still has one
item.description: As climate change accelerates, local experts say the date Wisconsin's Lake Mendota freezes over is getting later, making safe conditions for activities that rely on snow and ice harder to predict.
item.content:encoded:
As climate change accelerates, local experts say the date Wisconsin's Lake Mendota freezes over is getting later, making safe conditions for activities that rely on snow and ice harder to predict.

(Image credit: Kayla Wolf for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Berly McCoy

item.title: US military used laser to take down Border Protection drone, lawmakers say
item.description: The U.S. military used a laser to shoot down a Customs and Border Protection drone, members of Congress said Thursday, and the Federal Aviation Administration responded by closing more airspace near El Paso, Texas.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military used a laser to shoot down a Customs and Border Protection drone, members of Congress said Thursday, and the Federal Aviation Administration responded by closing more airspace near El Paso, Texas.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
